name: 56 · Isaac Experiment Capture (record_topics → persisted artifact + experiment metadata)
description: Phase 1 of the sim-first lab-capture arc: capture a robot experiment's raw signal record as a QUERYABLE catalogue artifact — no rosbag anywhere. A LeaseScope holds one `xarm_fleet` runner; its body forks (`parallel_split`) into (A) a `record_topics` ROS step that subscribes to BOTH sides of the Isaac hardware seam (`/isaac_joint_states` + `/isaac_joint_commands`) and streams every message as one timestamped NDJSON line `{t_ms, topic, msg}` onto a Data channel, and (B) the experiment itself — two FollowJointTrajectory motions (out to a working pose, back to ready). When the work branch finishes it publishes one `std_msgs/msg/Bool` to `/aithericon/record_stop`; the recorder (subscribed, same contract as demo 52's monitor stop) closes its stream within one event. The `capture_tee` consumer then drains the channel with the ordinary `for chunk in stream('recording')` API, persists the full record to an NDJSON file, and registers it via `log_artifact` with EXPERIMENT METADATA from the Start form (experiment_name / trial_id) — landing it in the data catalogue content-addressed, linked to this instance by provenance, and findable later via user-metadata containment (`GET /api/v1/catalogue?metadata={"experiment_name":…}`). Against the Isaac backend (`just dev isaac-runner-up`) the recorded `/isaac_joint_states` are REAL PhysX read-backs — the captured tracking error vs the commanded targets is the experiment outcome. LIVE ONLY: requires a running xarm stack (fake or Isaac); verify by launching a real instance, then querying the catalogue for the artifact.
